description: Element-type attribute rules applied in the intake-anchors scorer aggregation pass. Keyed by element_type from role library entries. Each rule returns a signed adjustment that the scorer adds to the candidate's total score. Total adjustment is capped to [-0.2, +0.15] per candidate to prevent any single rule from dominating the 0-1 base score range.

notes.adjustment_cap_rationale: Base scorer total sums to 1.0 exactly (0.25 size + 0.25 position + 0.2 text + 0.15 attr + 0.15 weight). An uncapped +0.2 or -0.3 would break the 0-1 assumption used by the 0.4 resolution threshold and the 0.5 cross-verify threshold. Cap at [-0.2, +0.15] preserves most of the signal while keeping the final score close to normalized.
notes.why_pass_not_uniform: Container and text rules are weaker signals so their adjustments are smaller. Price, heading, button are strong structural signals so they get the full cap.
notes.why_blocked_ancestors_matter: The v0.21 eval caught a wishlist-product blob winning detail_price on live because size_score dominated. blocked_ancestors + blocked_tags catch the common case where a blob custom element exposes price data as an attribute JSON blob instead of rendered text.
